CapGainsLossTxInvstIncmGrp.PropertyDesc: 1607 CAPITAL GLOBAL EX US FUND, LP

SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description1Txt: ARKANSAS SCHOOL FOR MATHEMATICS, SCIENCES, AND THE ARTS (ASMSA): LOAN TO RENOVATE TWO EXISTING STRUCTURES AT THIS RESIDENTIAL SELECTIVE HIGH SCHOOL FOR SOPHOMORE ENTRY.

ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.FormAndInfoAndMaterialsTxt: PLEASE VIIST OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON APPLICATION FORMS AND WHERE TO SUBMIT APPLICATIONS.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.SubmissionDeadlinesTxt: PLEASE VIIST OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON SUBMISSION DEADLINES.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.RestrictionsOnAwardsTxt: PLEASE VIIST OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON ELIGIBILITY OF AWARDS.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Scott County School - District Academic Magnet Hig

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: ADVANCING CHARLESTON EXCELLENCE (ACE) IS A ROLLING COHORT OF HIGH-ACHIEVING, LOW-INCOME SEVENTH- AND EIGHTH-GRADERS WHO MATRICULATE INTO ACADEMIC MAGNET HIGH SCHOOL DURING THE GRANT AND BEYOND. IN THE FALL 2020, A COHORT OF 117 LOW-INCOME, HIGH-ACHIEVING SEVENTH, EIGHTH, NINTH, AND TENTH GRADERS WILL MAKE UP THE ACE SCHOLARS COHORT. THROUGH THIS PROGRAM, WE ADDRESS BOTH THE ACCESS AND SUCCESS CHALLENGES THAT HINDER LOW-INCOME STUDENTS' PARTICIPATION IN SELECTIVE PUBLIC HIGH SCHOOLS.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Scott County School District - Mississippi Public

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: TO SUPPORT IMMEDIATE PROGRAM NEEDS AND COMPENSATION OF 20 COLLEGE TUTORS WHO CONDUCT REGULAR TUTORING SESSIONS FOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS INVOLVED IN THE CONSORTIUM'S AP STEM CURRICULA.

THE PURPOSE OF THIS GRANT IS TO SUPPORT SCHOLARSHIPS FOR YOUTHS TO ATTEND ATMTC ACADEMY'S SECONDARY THEATRE TRAINING CLASSES AND INTENSIVE SUMMER MUSICAL THEATRE TRAINING AND SUMMER STOCK PROGRAMMING.
RECLASSIFICATION OF CONFERENCE PARTICIPATION FEE TO DONATION BECAUSE OF CONFERENCE CANCELLATION DUE TO COVID19.

BSA/TWIGS WILL EXTEND THE NUMBER OF WEEKS FOR PROGRAM INSTRUCTION (FROM 20 TO 32) IN TWO DISCIPLINES, DANCE & INSTRUMENTAL MUSIC FOR THE 2020/21 PROGRAM YEAR. IN ORDER TO CREATE MORE EQUITABLE PATHWAYS FOR TWIGS STUDENTS IDENTIFIED AS LOW-INCOME, SEVERAL SUPPORT STRATEGIES WILL BE IMPLEMENTED. THE PRIMARY GOALS OF THIS WORK ARE TO INCREASE ENGAGEMENT AND HIGH SCHOOL ADMISSION RATE FOR LOW-INCOME 8TH GRADE TWIGS MUSICIANS.
TO SUPPORT THE 2020 NATIONAL RURAL EDUCATION SUMMIT IN SCOTTSDALE, AZ.
To support the 2020-21 edition of the Catalogue for Philanthropy: greater Washington.

COLLEGE TRACKS AIMS TO SERVE 60 LOW-TO-MODERATE INCOME STUDENTS IN MONTGOMERY COUNTY THROUGH THE ENHANCEMENT AND EXPANSION OF ITS HIGH-FLYER PROGRAM, DESIGNED TO IDENTIFY AND SERVE HIGH-ACHIEVING, FIRST-GENERATION-TO-COLLEGE STUDENTS TO ENSURE THEY APPLY AND ARE ACCEPTED TO HIGHLY-SELECTIVE COLLEGES AND SCHOLARSHIPS, AND SET THEM ON A COURSE FOR LIFELONG SUCCESS.
TO SUPPORT THE 2019 ACCEPT HACK THE GATES CONVENING.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Community Foundation For Loudoun And Northern Fauq

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the2019 Regional Philanthropy Summit, titled "The Equity Contiuum: Health, Education, & Justice".

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Community Foundation For Loudoun And Northern Fauq

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: TO SUPPORT PROGRAM EXPANSION ALLOWING THE PROGRAM TO SERVE UP TO 300 STUDENTS BY 2023. THE PROGRAM'S GOAL IS TO PREPARE STUDENTS ACADEMICALLY AND SOCIALLY TO SUCCESSFULLY APPLY TO AND COMPLETE COLLEGE THROUGH A SEVEN-YEAR PROGRAM WITH INTENSIVE ACADEMIC YEAR AND RESIDENTIAL ENRICHMENT COMPONENTS BEGINNING IN 10TH GRADE.
TO SUPPORT OUTREACH EFFORTS AND FEE WAIVERS FOR LOW-INCOME STUDENTS.

TO SUPPORT PROJECT LAUNCH, WHICH ENGAGES HIGH ABILITY STUDENTS WITH FINANCIAL NEED IN GRADES 5-7 IN ACADEMIC OPPORTUNITIES INCLUDING DUKE TIP'S SUMMER RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM, THE E-INVESTIGATORS ONLINE-LEARNING PROGRAM, BOOK CLUB EXPERIENCES, AND ABOVE-LEVEL TESTING.
To support a data-driven journalism and special commentary projects that will deeply investigate an important issue or challenge facing high-achieving students who have financial need.

TO SUPPORT THE EXPANSION OF LINK UP FOR TITLE 1 SCHOOL STUDENTS IN GRADES THREE TO FIVE. UNITING THE CLASSROOM WITH THE CONCERT HALL, STUDENTS EXPLORE MUSIC THROUGH A RESEARCH-BASED CURRICULUM OF CLASSROOM ACTIVITIES OVER 4-6 MONTHS, CULMINATING IN A CONCERT WHERE STUDENTS PERFORM WITH THE FAIRFAX SYMPHONY.

THIS GRANT WILL SUPPORT FORD'S THEATRE SOCIETY'S STAND UP: BE HEARD ORATORY PROGRAM, FOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS IN THE WASHINGTON, D.C. AREA. BY INTEGRATING ORATORY AND PERFORMANCE INTO A STUDENT'S EDUCATIONAL EXPERIENCE, THIS PROGRAM ENABLES STUDENTS TO LEARN HOW PUBLIC SPEAKING CAN HELP THEM COMMUNICATE IDEAS AND INSPIRE OTHERS.

THE PURPOSE OF THIS FIELD EXPERIENCE IS TO PROVIDE CLARKE COUNTY HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS WITH A 4-DAY IMMERSION INTO CHESAPEAKE BAY ECOLOGY, ECONOMY, CAREERS, AND CULTURE TO SHOWCASE CONNECTIONS BETWEEN THEIR CHESAPEAKE BAY HEADWATERS REGION AND THE BAY ESTUARY EXPOSING THEM TO AGRICULTURE THEY NEVER WOULD HAVE EXPERIENCED OTHERWISE.
To support Jack Kent Cooke Young Artist Awards

to support ADVANCE - a partnership between Northern Virginia Community College and George Mason University designed to streamline the transfer process for community college students interested in pursuing a bachelor's degree. Foundation's grant will support scholarships for approximately 20 high achieving students with financial need who matriculate into George Mason University after completing their associate's degree.

To support EIP's new program enhancements that will focus on increasing student exposure to and preparation for George Mason's Honors College and to provide additional scholarships for students who enroll in Honors College.

TO SUPPORT THE ARTREACH GW PORTFOLIO DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM, WHICH PROVIDES OPPORTUNITIES FOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS LIVING IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES OF DC WARDS 7, 8, AND NEARBY PRINCE GEORGE'S COUNTY TO RECEIVE ADVANCED VISUAL ARTS TRAINING, PORTFOLIO DEVELOPMENT, AND EXHIBITION DESIGN EXPERIENCE, INCREASING ACCESS TO ARTS EDUCATION AND COLLEGE ADMISSION PREPARATION.

RecipientUSAddress.AddressLine1Txt: 37th and O Streets NW PO Box 57125

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: TO SUPPORT THE 2019 GRANTMAKERS FOR EDUCATION ANNUAL CONFERENCE, TAKING PLACE OCTOBER 22 - 24 IN NEW ORLEANS, LOUISIANA.

TO SUPPORT PROJECT PURPLE, WHICH WILL IDENTIFY AND SUPPORT APPROXIMATELY 60 MOTIVATED AND HIGH PERFORMING MIDDLE SCHOOL STUDENTS OF LIMITED ECONOMIC MEANS AT TWO CHICAGO PUBLIC SCHOOL SITES. EACH SITE WILL OPERATE A TWO-YEAR, TUITION-FREE PROGRAM THAT WILL INFUSE ENRICHMENT PROGRAMMING FROM NORTHWESTERN UNIVERSITY INTO HIGH JUMP'S EXISTING MODEL.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Johns Hopkins University Center for Talented Youth

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support intentional outreach to identify qualified students with financial need, program enhancements, and direct student scholarships for approximately 120 participants for the Pathways to College program. While it is unclear if in-person programming will resume in the fall due to COVID-19, CTY is prepared to offer a virtual Pathways to College curriculum.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Johns Hopkins University Center for Talented Youth

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: THE SUPPORT A NEW ACADEMIC ADVISING PROGRAM THAT WILL ADDRESS THE VAST INCONSISTENCIES IN MIDDLE AND HIGH SCHOOL ACCESS AND PLACEMENT AND LEVEL THE PLAYING FIELD BY MAKING THE SCHOOL CHOICE LANDSCAPE TRANSPARENT AND MORE FULLY ACCESSIBLE TO ACADEMICALLY ADVANCED STUDENTS IN LOW-RESOURCE COMMUNITIES.

TO SUPPORT THE EXPANSION OF THE LEVEL UP PROGRAM INTO TWO ADDITIONAL MIDDLE SCHOOLS, AND RESTRUCTURING THE EXISTING LEVEL UP PROGRAM AT TWO MIDDLE SCHOOLS TO TAKE PLACE DURING THE RESOURCE BLOCK TO ACCOMMODATE AN INCREASED NUMBER OF PARTICIPATING STUDENTS.

TO SUPPORT COLLEGE VISITS FOR THE AVID STUDENTS AND WILL INCREASE COLLEGE AND UNIVERSITY ENROLLMENT BY A POPULATION OF STUDENTS THAT IS TRADITIONALLY UNDERSERVED. TOURS WILL ACCOMMODATE APPROXIMATELY ALL 295 AVID STUDENTS.

TO SUPPORT EXCELLENCE IN EDUCATION BANQUETS CELEBRATING ACADEMIC ACHIEVEMENT OF STUDENTS (THE TOP 5-PERCENT OF THEIR CLASS ACADEMICALLY) AND SUPPORT SELECTION OF NATIONAL MERIT SCHOLARSHIP SEMIFINALISTS AND NATIONAL HISPANIC RECOGNITION SCHOLARS. MORE THAN 1,000 PEOPLE PARTICIPATE IN THIS ANNUAL RECOGNITION EVENT.

TO SUPPORT MATRICULATE'S ORGANIZATIONAL CAPACITY TO SCALE ITS SYSTEMS AND OPERATIONS, ESTABLISH INNOVATION AS PART OF ITS WORK, AND DEVELOP REVENUE MODELS TO REACH SCALE. DURING THE GRANT PERIOD, MATRICULATE WILL SERVE OVER 4,000 HIGH-ACHIEVING STUDENTS WHO HAVE FINANCIAL NEED, EMPOWERING THEM ATTEND THE NATION'S TOP COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES.

THE MORVEN PARK CENTER FOR CIVIC IMPACT (MPCCI) PROVIDES FREE, MEANINGFUL CIVICS EDUCATION LESSONS TO LOCAL STUDENTS. FUNDING WILL SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OF FOUR NEW LESSONS, TRANSPORTATION FOR UNDERSERVED STUDENTS, SUPPLIES FOR SUMMER ENRICHMENT PROGRAMS, AND DIGITIZATION OF PROGRAM MATERIALS TO REACH 13,000 LOUDOUN COUNTY PUBLIC SCHOOL (LCPS) STUDENTS.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: National Council For Community And Education Partn
Northern Virginia Community College Educational Fo

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To facilitate the successful college transition, retention and completion of high-achieving students with financial need in the Pathway to the Baccalaureate Program at Northern Virginia Community College, through a combination of college counseling services and scholarships.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Northern Virginia Community College Educational Fo
Northwestern University Center for Talent Developm

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To increase the number of students who have access to above-grade-level assessment and talent development resources. To ensure that high-ability students with financial need discovered through the talent search receive supplemental enrichment and accelerated programming as early as possible. To continue to nurture and maintain long-term partnerships with under-resourced schools, school districts, community organizations, and parents to build their capacity for meeting the needs of their high-ability students

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Northwestern University Center for Talent Developm

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: TO SUPPORT THE EXPANSION OF PROJECT OCCAMS TO ADDITIONAL SCHOOLS, THE DEVELOPMENT OF AN ONLINE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM AND IMPLEMENTATION TOOLKIT, AND THE PILOTING OF ONLINE CURRICULUM VIA GIFTED LEARNING LINKS.
TO SUPPORT PEAK GENERAL OPERATING EXPENSES.

THE GOAL IF THIS PROGRAM IS TO EXPOSE HIGH ACHIEVING LOW INCOME STUDENTS FROM ACROSS LOUDOUN COUNTY AND PARENTS TO A TRUE COLLEGE EXPERIENCE. BOTH STUDENTS AND THEIR PARENTS WOULD BE ABLE TO EARN COLLEGE CREDIT AND BECOME COLLEGE READY.
SPONSORSHIP OF THE 2020 AMERICAN JOURNEY AWARDS DINNER.

THE SCHOLARSHIP FUND OF ALEXANDRIA REQUESTS FUNDING FOR A PILOT PROGRAM TO HELP 25 HIGH-ACHIEVING, LOW-INCOME, AND/OR FIRST-GENERATION JUNIORS WHO HAVE THE POTENTIAL TO BE ACCEPTED INTO HIGHLY SELECTIVE UNIVERSITIES BUT DO NOT HAVE THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE COLLEGE SELECTION, APPLICATION AND FUNDING PROCESS TO DETERMINE THEIR BEST MATCH COLLEGES.

TO SUPPORT THE ADVOCATE GRANT PROGRAM, ENSURING THAT UNDERREPRESENTED STUDENTS SUBMIT HIGH QUALITY APPLICATIONS TO SCIENTIFIC COMPETITIONS AND TO RAISE THE NUMBER OF UNDERREPRESENTED STUDENTS WHO COMPETE FOR AND WIN PRESTIGIOUS AWARDS.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Art of Problem Solving Initiative Inc

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: TO PROVIDE NON-RESIDENTIAL, FIVE-WEEK SUMMER PROGRAMS AT TWO SITES IN NEW YORK CITY TO APPROXIMATELY 200 UNDERSERVED 6TH GRADE STUDENTS FROM NEW YORK CITY PUBLIC SCHOOLS WHO DEMONSTRATE TALENT IN MATH IN ORDER TO DEVELOP THEIR PASSION FOR MATH AND PREPARE THEM FOR ADVANCED STUDY IN MATHEMATICS.

ADVANCING THE TRANSFER PREP ACADEMY SCHOLARS PROGRAM. TO SUPPORT THE PROJECT MANAGEMENT OF THE TWO-YEAR PILOT PROGRAM - A NATIONAL COMMUNITY COLLEGE TRANSFER PIPELINE TO SELECTIVE INSTITUTIONS THAT MEET THE ACADEMIC ASPIRATIONS AND FINANCIAL NEEDS OF HIGH-ACHIEVING STUDENTS.

TO SUPPORT HIGH-QUALITY STORIES ON THE OBSTACLES FACED BY HIGH-ACHIEVING STUDENTS WITH FINANCIAL NEED AND ANALYSIS ABOUT THE INEQUALITIES THAT PERSIST IN HIGHER EDUCATION - RAISING AWARENESS AND HIGHLIGHTING SOLUTIONS TO THESE DISPARITIES.

TO SUPPORT THE IMPLEMENTATION OF A COMPREHENSIVE DATA SYSTEM THAT WILL ALLOW INGENUITY TO GATHER INFORMATION FROM STUDENTS AND FAMILIES AT PIVOTAL MILESTONES AND TRANSITIONS IN THE PROGRAM TO SURFACE FACTORS INFLUENCING STUDENT WELL-BEING, IDENTITY, AND RETENTION/ACADEMIC PROGRESS. DATA COLLECTION, BOTH QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE, WILL HELP THE SCHOOL UNDERSTAND INCOME-RELATED GAPS AND IMPLEMENT POLICIES, SYSTEMS, AND PROGRAMMING THAT MEET STUDENT NEED AND LEVEL THE PLAYING FIELD FOR STUDENTS WHO HAVE FINANCIAL NEED.

TO SPONSOR TEAK FELLOWSHIP'S 3RD ANNUAL COLLEGE SUCCESS SUMMIT ON SATURDAY, MAY 30, 2020. THE COLLEGE SUCCESS SUMMIT IS DESIGNED TO BUILD COMMUNITY, FOSTER PARTNERSHIPS, AND TO PROVIDE CRITICAL LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES TO FURTHER SUPPORT HIGHLY MOTIVATED, LOW-INCOME, FIRST-GENERATION COLLEGE STUDENTS.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Young Center For Immigrant Childrens Rights

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support a one-day convening for Community College Transfer Advisers and a two-day Community College Transfer Boot Camp for strong prospective applicants to selective institutions.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Trustees of the University of Pennsylvania
University of Maryland Baltimore County Foundation
University Of Maryland College Park Foundation Inc
Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State Universit

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To address a major opportunity gap, the Appalachian Rural Talent Initiative would extend key components from a federal grant by increasing the number of students eligible for gifted education services and creating sustainable gifted programming in high-poverty rural Appalachian schools, with a goal of reaching 500 or more under-served students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State Universit

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the pilot of a new residential summer enrichment experience for approximately 80-100 gifted rural students in grades 6-7 living in high-poverty rural Appalachian communities.
To sponsor the 2020 Race, Equity and the Future of Greater Washington Regional Summit on June 11, 2020 in Washington, D.C.

TO ACQUIRE KODABLE COMPUTER SCIENCE CURRICULUM, COMPUTER HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE, AND SEED IMPLEMENTATION OF YMCA LOUDOUN TECHNOLOGY & CODING CLUB. THE CLUB IS A COMPUTER SCIENCE INITIATIVE THAT WILL INSTILL CREATIVITY, PROBLEM SOLVING, AND CRITICAL THINKING SKILLS, AND THE PERSEVERANCE NEEDED FOR 40 YOUTH TO SUCCEED IN ACADEMIA AND LIFE.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Friends School of Baltimore - Baltimore Children o

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: Matching Gifts - Scholarship and Education Grants

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: University of Maryland Baltimore County Foundation

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: Matching Gifts - Scholarship and Education Grants

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: FUND THE OPENING AND OPERATION OF 3 ART OF PROBLEM SOLVING ACADEMY LEARNING CENTERS
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: HPL submitted semi-annual reports of program impact through December 31, 2020. HPL also submitted quarterly financial reports through June 30, 2020 reflecting the liability for this PRI and confirming that funds were used for center openings, operation, and scholarships for high performing math students with financial need. HPL is in compliance with the PRI terms and repayment schedule. The outstanding PRI loan balance is $381,780.

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: provide high-achieving students with financial need access to affordable student loans
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.DatesOfReportsByGranteeTxt: Last report received was for period ending June 30,2019
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: The PRI obligation to the filing organization was released by amending the loan agreement which now stipulates that the grantee would satisfy the terms of the PRI loan via student loan debt forgiveness. All reasonable and appropriate steps are being taken to ensure that the PRI loan funds are being utilized by the grantee for intended PRI purposes. The filing organization will not be providing any further payments to the grantee.
